tasks.title: Add atomic task-claim mechanism so agents cannot concurrently select the same task
tasks.notes: tasks.json is a flat file with a single-writer assumption. Two agents selecting the same task produce last-write-wins corruption that T23 drift detection catches only after damage is done. The fix is an atomic claim step: an agent writes a claim record before execution begins, the control plane validates the claim is still current before reconciling the completion report, and stale or contested claims are surfaced in preflight.

The only reason a human is involved is that Ralph has no mechanism to trigger an extension host restart itself. VS Code exposes vscode.commands.executeCommand('workbench.action.reloadWindow') which restarts the extension host without closing the window. In loop mode this stop reason should auto-trigger that command after persisting the iteration result, rather than surfacing it as an operator decision point. This is the lowest-risk human-removal change in the codebase because the state is already clean when the reload fires.

tasks.title: Add withStateLock to stateManager.ts â€” a state.lock file mechanism wrapping every saveState call
tasks.notes: In src/ralph/stateManager.ts, add a withStateLock function modelled on withTaskFileLock in src/ralph/taskFile.ts: acquire an exclusive wx-open on state.lock in the same directory as state.json; retry up to lockRetryCount times with lockRetryDelayMs delay; return a lock_timeout result if the lock cannot be acquired; always release in a finally block. Wrap every saveState call site in stateManager.ts inside withStateLock so no state.json mutation can proceed without holding the lock. The lock is not held across the entire iteration â€” only for the duration of each read-modify-write cycle. Update docs/invariants.md to document the state.lock contract alongside the existing tasks.lock section.

tasks.title: Atomic nextIteration allocation â€” acquire the state lock and increment nextIteration before any artifact paths are generated
tasks.notes: In src/ralph/iterationPreparation.ts the iteration number is currently read from snapshot.state.nextIteration â€” a snapshot captured before any lock is held, so two concurrent agents can read the same value. Change the flow so the very first step of prepareIterationContext (or a new allocateIteration helper it calls) acquires the state lock, reads the live state.json, increments nextIteration, writes it back, and releases the lock â€” returning the freshly allocated number. All artifact path construction must use this allocated number, not the pre-lock snapshot value. The allocation write is intentionally minimal (only nextIteration changes) so the lock is held for the shortest possible time.

tasks.title: Session handoff protocol â€” serialize mid-task context on loop termination so the next session resumes without re-reading full iteration history
tasks.notes: Ralph loops terminate cleanly for many reasons that are not failures: context window exhaustion, control_plane_reload_required, manual stop, iteration cap. Currently the next session re-reads the full iteration history to reconstruct context. Gastown's gt handoff model solves this: on clean termination, serialize a structured handoff note capturing what was done and what remains, so the next session picks up precisely where the previous one stopped â€” without re-deriving context from raw artifacts. This is the complement to T32 (auto-reload): T32 handles the trigger, T41 handles the context carry-over.

tasks.title: Orchestration integration smoke test: exercise graph + handoffs + role policies + fan-in/fan-out end-to-end
tasks.notes: Added opt-in orchestration E2E smoke (scripts/run-e2e-orchestration-smoke.js) and test harness that exercise graph + fan-in + handoff + role policy + scope_expansion human gate; wired test:e2e-orchestration npm script and documented it in docs/testing.md.
acceptance: An opt-in test:e2e-orchestration npm script (guarded behind RALPH_E2E_ORCHESTRATION=1) seeds a temp workspace, initializes an orchestration graph with at least one fanout/fanin wave, advances the graph through a stub agent execution, and asserts that (a) graph state.json is persisted, (b) parent task does not complete until fan-in gates are satisfied, (c) node span artifacts are written
Smoke test exercises at least one handoff lifecycle (propose->accept->complete) and asserts the handoff record transitions correctly
Smoke test exercises role policy enforcement: a reviewer-role agent attempt to emit a disallowed action produces a policy_violation stop reason
Human choke point gate artifact is written when scope_expansion threshold is crossed in the stub execution
docs/testing.md documents the new opt-in script alongside test:e2e-pipeline
npm run validate passes

tasks.title: Dashboard orchestration panel: surface orchestration graph state, node spans, re-plan history, and fan-in gate status
tasks.notes: Dashboard Orchestration panel implemented: added OrchestrationPanelSection to dashboardSnapshot, extended collectStatusSnapshot to collect human gate artifacts/fanInRecord/nodeSpans, added stateWatcher for orchestration files, added Orchestration tab in panelHtml, 8 regression tests added. npm run validate passes (1021/1021 tests).
acceptance: The Ralphdex dashboard includes an Orchestration tab (or expandable section) that renders: current graph node, prior node outcomes (from node span artifacts), pending branch nodes, and fan-in gate open/closed/failed status
Re-plan artifacts (from replanDecisionPath) are listed with trigger evidence summary and resulting task-graph diff so operators can audit why a replan was triggered
Human gate artifacts (scope_expansion, dependency_rewiring, contested_fan_in_scm) appear in the orchestration panel with Approve/Reject actions wired through ralphCodex.approveHumanReview
Panel refreshes on filesystem changes to the orchestration state artifacts (state.json, span files, human-gate files) so it stays live during an active run
At least 5 regression tests cover the new orchestration panel rendering for empty state, active graph, and human-gate-pending states
npm run validate passes
